title: Ability for admins to delete private dashboards when owner/members left the company

posts.author: DeWinter (De Winter)
posts.cookedHtml:
Description

Atm there is no possibility as an admin to delete private dashboards. When someone leaves the company and they created a private dashboard there is no way for an admin to get rid of it.

What are you trying to achieve

Add the ability for admins to delete private dashboards

posts.author: ProjectMastermind (IngenuityKat)
posts.cookedHtml:
This actually is possible today, just a bit hidden.

As an admin you can:

  • Go to Content Directory

  • Find the private dashboard

  • Click the three dots

  • Add yourself as owner

  • Then delete it

posts.author: lAMhUAN21 (hUAN)
posts.cookedHtml: Completely agree with this. From an admin perspective, it creates unnecessary clutter and no way to properly clean things up when ownership is lost. Even a limited admin override for private dashboards would solve a lot of headaches.

posts.author: DeWinter (De Winter)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi.. it’'s not possible. For regular boards it is but not for private dashboards. The options with the three dots (Archive and Delete) are greyed out.

posts.author: ProjectMastermind (IngenuityKat)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey—might be worth double-checking your admin permissions and reaching out to monday support. I’m able to manage/delete private dashboards on my account, so something might be off on the permissions side. See screenshots below.

posts.cookedHtml: Hi, thnx for sharing. I am now triple checking with monday as your screen shot seems promising!
